issues.title: Migrate Counter usecase from `commonware-avs-router` to this repo
issues.author: bagelface
issues.body:
### Description

The `commonware-avs-router` repo currently includes the Counter usecase implementation, which adds unnecessary application-specific code to what should remain a reusable library.

Move the Counter-related code (including logic, tests, and related configs) into this repo. The router repo should only contain the shared library code.

### Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Counter usecase fully migrated to `commonware-avs-counter`
- [ ] `commonware-avs-router` contains only reusable library components
- [ ] Update any references or documentation as needed

### Example

Example directory structure for the updated repo:

```
commonware-avs-counter/
├── contracts/               # Solidity source code
│   ├── Counter.sol
│   └── interfaces/
│       └── ICounter.sol
│
├── rust/                    # Rust workspace for off-chain logic
│   ├── Cargo.toml
│   ├── src/
│   │   ├── main.rs          # entrypoint for CLI or binary
│   │   ├── lib.rs           # shared Rust library logic
│   │   ├── bindings.rs      # generated Solidity bindings (e.g. via ethers-rs or abigen)
│   │   └── usecase/
│   │       └── mod.rs
│   └── tests/
│       └── integration.rs
│
├── scripts/                 # Scripts for building, deploying, testing
│   ├── deploy.ts
│   ├── test.ts
│   └── utils/
│       └── encodeData.ts
│
├── test/                    # Solidity/Hardhat or Foundry tests
│   ├── Counter.t.sol
│   └── Router.t.sol
│
├── foundry.toml             # Foundry config
├── hardhat.config.ts        # (optional) if using Hardhat for deployment
├── Cargo.toml               # optional workspace-level Cargo config
├── package.json             # JS/TS dependencies if using scripts
├── README.md
└── LICENSE
```

Yes,

So as a grantee, you can choose to request Builder OP or User incentives. Each has its own characteristics and rules.

**Builder OP** is the amount of OP that one receives after 12 months if the grant is completed the builder can choose what to do with it once they receive it.

**User incentive OP** is an OP amount a grant receives that is planning to incorporate a user incentive program like points, TVL incentives, etc. This OP is transferrable and can only be used for the incentive program and is not allowed to pay the team budget.

**Note:** A grant can be requested both at the same time for rewarding the work and for incentivizing users.

issues.title: obUSD Spec
issues.author: RonTuretzky

comments.body: @RonTuretzky can you list and make clear the two schemas used for this project?
@diterra-code please be sure to have references to the schemas used in documentation if not already done.

issues.body:
(Optimism Builder's Dollar Referred to as obUSD here after)
## Overview 

The goal of obUSD (prev. PGFDAI) is to allow Projects/teams that have been approved for builder grants by the Optimism Grants council to be eligible for receiving funding while completing their grants. This is conditional on them receiving a minimum number of vouches from accredited Optimism ecosystem members. The funding is generated by the total holdings of obUSD, which generates and redistributes yield to eligible projects. obUSD is an alternative to holding stablecoins (such as USDC/USDT) and can be held by the optimism foundation, builders and projects in the space. 
## Background 
**Yield Generation**
Yield is generated by supplying DAI liquidity to AAVE, which earns interest on supplying and borrowing assets with a variable interest rates.  This allows for a risk free source of yield, preserving the stablecoin aspects of the asset, while generating yield for the builders. 
Other risk free sources of yield were considered, and are potential candidates for future versions and upgrades, but for the initial version a tried and true method was selected. 

Please refer to [this file](https://github.com/BreadchainCoop/bread-token/blob/main/contracts/Bread.sol) for the source code of the an existing implementation utilizing the same mechanism which was [deployed to mainnet](https://polygonscan.com/token/0x11d9efdf4ab4a3bfabf5c7089f56aa4f059aa14c?a=0xE41D784991067704186984Ed06d8e04bd5aD0fc2) and utilized by the Breadchain community.

**EAS**
The Optimism foundation utilizes [EAS](https://attest.org/) to identify projects , project applications and more. The obUSD mechanism will utilize these attestations in order to integrate mechanism into the Optimism eco-system. 
**Project Grantee Configuration**
Attestations provided by the Optimism foundation will be submitted to the smart contract infrastructure, validating the authenticity of the attestation. Given a valid attestation , the infrastructure

issues.author: lirona
issues.body:
this function [returns the final amount actually withdrawn](https://aave.com/docs/developers/smart-contracts/pool#write-methods-withdraw-return-values), and should be checked when called that it is the amount expected, or burn the corresponding amount of BuilderDollar.

the [current aave-v3 contracts](https://github.com/aave/aave-v3-core/blob/782f51917056a53a2c228701058a6c3fb233684a/contracts/protocol/libraries/logic/SupplyLogic.sol#L124) allow for the requested amount to be either within the user's balance or `type(uint256).max`, which will fail the burning of `BuilderDollar`, but still this value should be checked as this might change in future versions.

issues.title: make `BuilderDollar` Pausable
issues.author: lirona

comments.body: I don't see the purpose of adding Pausable

@daopunk this is important in case something goes wrong so we can pause the contract and only the owner can interact with it. maybe it gets hacked, or some vulnerability is discovered and we want to stop minting and burning until we fix the issue, maybe the dao/owners decide to pause usage for some time.. it's a good safety mechanism to have, especially for a token, and especially since we are interacting with external contracts here. maybe the pool's contract is acting in an unexpected way and than we would want to pause interactions with it and investigate.
my main concern is the centralization of control. i understand it can mitigate risk in one area, but it introduces risk in another area.

issues.body:
- add a pauser role or give pausing permissions to owner
- add `whenNotPaused` modifier to `mint` and `burn` functions

issues.title: Grow Kesennuma Spec
issues.author: RonTuretzky
issues.body:
## Background 

Kesennuma, a coastal city in Miyagi Prefecture, Japan, has been the focus of various local impact funding initiatives aimed at rebuilding and revitalizing the community following the devastation of the 2011 Great East Japan Earthquake and Tsunami.
##### Targeted Funding Initiatives

Several funding programs have been instrumental in Kesennuma's recovery:

- **Kesennuma Study**: Launched in 2019, this participatory action research project focuses on preventing frailty among older adults by promoting social participation through community salons. The study aims to delay the need for long-term care in areas affected by the disaster .[PMC](https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC9643787/?utm_source=chatgpt.com)
    
- **Community Impact Fund**: Managed by the Central Community Chest of Japan, this fund supports private social welfare enterprises, NPOs, and citizen activity groups. Grants of up to 10 million yen per year are awarded to projects that address social issues, such as child poverty and community revitalization .[赤い羽根共同募金](https://www.akaihane.or.jp/english/fund/?utm_source=chatgpt.com)
    
- **World Monuments Fund (WMF)**: Through the "Save Our Culture" initiative, WMF raised funds to assist local residents and business owners in their recovery efforts. This included the restoration of Kesennuma's historic cityscape, contributing to both cultural preservation and community revitalization .[presentations.thebestinheritage.com+1World Monuments Fund+1](https://presentations.thebestinheritage.com/2022/kesennuma-historic-citiscape?utm_source=chatgpt.com)
    
- **US-Japan Foundation Grants**: In April 2021, the foundation awarded a grant to support earthquake and tsunami-devastated populations in Kesennuma. The project involved partnering with an international data company and local government to raise employment opportunities by creating training centers for programming and data input .

## Motivation 

The application of cryptocurrency and bloc

issues.title: Prevent users from sending Bread tokens to the token contract address (address(this))
issues.author: RonTuretzky
issues.body:
## Problem
Currently, users can mistakenly send Bread tokensdirectly to the Bread token contract address using , which results in the tokens being lost or locked. This is a common issue in ERC20 contracts where users may not realize that sending tokens to the contract address is unsafe.

## Requirements
- Prevent users from sending Bread tokens or ETH to the Bread token contract address (i.e., using `address(this)`).
- Add logic to revert or prevent such transactions.
- Provide user feedback (via revert messages) if a transfer is attempted to the token contract address.
- Update relevant documentation and tests.

## Proposed Solution
- Implement a check in the `transfer` and `transferFrom` functions to revert if the recipient address is `address(this)`.
- Implement a `receive()` and/or `fallback()` function that reverts to prevent accidental ETH transfers.

## Sequence Diagram
```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
    participant User
    participant BreadTokenContract
    User->>BreadTokenContract: transfer(address(this), amount)
    BreadTokenContract-->>User: revert("Cannot send to token contract address")

    User->>BreadTokenContract: send ETH to address(this)
    BreadTokenContract-->>User: revert("Cannot send ETH to token contract address")
```

## Solidity Code Snippet
```solidity
// Example for ERC20 transfer and transferFrom prevention
function transfer(address to, uint256 amount) public override returns (bool) {
    require(to != address(this), "Cannot send to token contract address");
    return super.transfer(to, amount);
}

function transferFrom(address from, address to, uint256 amount) public override returns (bool) {
    require(to != address(this), "Cannot send to token contract address");
    return super.transferFrom(from, to, amount);
}

```

## Acceptance Criteria
- Attempting to transfer tokens to the contract address should revert with a clear error message.
- Documentation and tests are updated to reflect this behavior.
